_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD/MS/ DNB/ PG Diploma or equivalent (in Ophthalmology)
clinical_indications: Removal of scar tissue from retina
Removal of foreign matter from the vitreous humor (e.g., in diabetic vitreous hemorrhage)
Removal of vision blocking vitreous opacities

claim.label: Sticker/Barcode of the Intraocular lens (IOL) (If IOL Used)
Intraoperative photograph with patient ID, time and date stamp (optional)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical examination suggests presence of vitreoretinal indications for performing surgery such as vitreous haemorrhage/ vitreous opacities, retinal detachment, retinal tear, diabetic retinopathy, etc.?

Detailed Clinical notes including history (diabetes/trauma), clinical symptoms (visual haze/ floaters/ cloudy vision/ photophobia/ shadow/ curtain effect/ cobwebs) and examination findings (fundus examination/B-scan (optional)) are present?
Clinical photo of Affected Eye (R or L) with full face photograph is provided?

cpd_claim.q: OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed (including steps indication insertion of silicon oil) and the outcomes of surgery?
Documents show appropriate post-op care and follow-up advice?

common_queries: Clinical examination does not suggest presence of vitreoretinal indications for performing surgery (e.g., vitreous haemorrhage, vitreous opacities, retinal detachment, retinal tear, diabetic retinopathy).

Clinical notes incomplete regarding detailed history (diabetes/trauma), clinical symptoms (visual haze/ floaters/ cloudy vision/ photophobia/ shadow/ curtain effect/ cobwebs), or examination findings (fundus examination).
Affected eye not labelled R or L in the clinical photograph, or full face photograph not provided.
Procedure/operative notes missing at claim submission or lack detail on surgical steps and outcomes.
IOL Sticker/Barcode not provided at claim stage, despite IOL being used.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, ophthalmic examination.
surgical_options: Pars plana vitrectomy (for diseases of the posterior segment)
Anterior vitrectomy (rare, may happen in cases following eye trauma, during complex cataract, cornea and glaucoma surgery or lens problems)
complications_of_vitreoretinal_surgery_s: Acute and chronic changes in intraocular pressure (IOP)
investigations: Fundus examination using direct/ indirect ophthalmoscope
